content:
{
  "title": "Error-Aware Prompting to Enhance Code Generation in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Existing prompting methods for code generation often fail to guide LLMs away from common coding errors, resulting in syntactic or logical mistakes in generated outputs. There is a need for prompt designs that actively reduce error rates.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly used for programming tasks, improving reliability and correctness is crucial. Traditional prompts do not leverage error patterns that LLMs tend to make, missing an opportunity to guide generation with counterexamples. A novel prompting strategy that leverages prior errors could yield more robust code generation.",
  "method": "We propose 'Error-Aware Prompting,' where each prompt includes a short incorrect code snippet, its error message, and a corrected version, followed by the new coding instruction. The hypothesis is that exposing the LLM to recent error/correction pairs sensitizes it to avoid similar mistakes in subsequent generations. This method can be easily constructed from datasets containing code submissions and associated test results, without model retraining.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on the HumanEval and MBPP datasets, comparing standard instruction-only prompts to error-aware prompts. Metrics will include pass@1, exact match, and syntactic correctness. The key test is whether error-aware prompts reduce the rate of repeat error types and improve functional correctness over baselines."
}
